kept.text:
Time to time, the downstream effects of every major emerging sector in DeFi have always been amplified through @pendle_fi.

With V2 yield tokenisation enabling:

🔹 Yield discovery via YT trading
🔹 A foundational fixed-yield primitive through PTs → forming the basis for layered DeFi strategies

The core value proposition and playbook have remained  consistent.

And that consistency is precisely why Pendle continues to command such a strong market dominance moat.

So now you ask, why Pendle?

The answer is more than obvious. It financialises yield to it's most expressive form that creates an incredibly powerful flywheel:

1⃣New yield sectors emerge
2⃣Pendle becomes the discovery + financialisation venue
3⃣ liquidity deepens
4⃣ integrations expand
5⃣ more strategies become possible
6⃣ more capital flows back into Pendle (repeat)

This is The Pendle Effect.

Pendle

kept.text:
Peptoma Top Contributor Challenge Season 1 is LIVE 🏆

We’ve added 20,000,000 $PEPTM into the rewards pool.
7 days. Proportional distribution. Every contribution counts.

How to earn your share:

🧪 Submit a peptide sequence to The Lab → +10 pts
💬 Annotate a discovery (confirm / challenge / extend / tag) → +15 pts

The more you contribute relative to everyone else, the bigger your slice of the pool.

Reward formula:
Your reward = (your score / total score of all participants) × 20,000,000 $PEPTM

No fixed winner. No cutoff. Every participant who contributes gets rewarded.

Event window: May 6 – May 13, 2026
Leaderboard updates every 10 seconds live at https://t.co/toZuWe9czj

How to participate:

→ Go to https://t.co/vGHIIZALtG
→ Connect your Solana wallet
→ Head to The Lab and submit a peptide sequence
→ Go to the Feed and annotate discoveries from other researchers
→ Watch your score and estimated rewards update in real time

$PEPTM · Solana · Open DeSci · Season 1

kept.text:
one thing I wish I knew when I started LPing was

be versatile, and put the time in to stay locked in.

back in late 2024 and early 2025, i mostly used $1k–$1.5k liquidity, and i set a target for myself to make at least $60–$120 per day. looking back, i realized that approach was completely wrong

most of the time, i was only securing 1 – 3% per position, while risking losses of 10 – 20% if things went wrong

another mistake was: only using one threshold to filter the tokens i played. market environments constantly change, and when i couldn’t adapt or adjust my thresholds, i stopped making money consistently

now, i challenge myself to use as little SOL as possible while targeting at least 1 SOL daily, or around 30 – 50% profit from my average entry

this completely changed my approach to LPing. losses don’t hurt as much as they used to, and i genuinely feel like my LP skillset has improved

being versatile means understanding the current market environment, adapting your playstyle, and changing the thresholds of the tokens you LP so you can keep generating profits daily instead of waiting for volume to come

obviously, it took time. i had to put my fear and ego aside to realize all of this

but I wouldn’t have reached this point without the grind sessions and people from @EnclaviumFNF, @met_lparmy, Bismillah DAWO, and @MeteoraIDN

kept.text:
Discovery is challenging. 

You don’t realize you already have what it takes until you’re there.

profile_bio.description: Content Creator • Writer • Animator📝 | Amb @Arbitrum | Enterpreneur

kept.text:
the entire SEO industry has been running guesswork tests on AI search for 9 months

while Google has been openly selling the algorithm as enterprise software

it's called Google Cloud Discovery Engine

we've used what's in this documentation to drive over $30M in client revenue

inside that documentation are the 7 actual ranking signals powering AI mode rn

base ranking, Gecko embeddings, Jetstream, BM25, PCTR, freshness, boost-and-bury

but the actual unlock is buried deeper

Google chunks every page into smaller pieces before indexing it

not many people realise this

and every chunk carries its ancestor headings with it

which means an H3 that says "conclusion" gets indexed with ZERO context

an H3 that says "conclusion on app monetization ROI in 2025" gets indexed with full keyword + semantic relevance

the SEO agencies billing you $5k/month to "test and learn" never opened the doc

they couldn't, because reading it would expose that the past 9 months of their work was performance art

meanwhile Google also runs query fan-out

a single search like "how to monetize an app" gets broken into 5+ sub-queries internally

subscription models, in-app purchases, ad revenue, premium tiers, retention strategy

then synthesizes content from different pages for each one

so if your competitor has 5 connected pages and you have 1 pillar post, they're feeding 5 sub-queries and you're feeding zero

this is why some sites get cited 3x in a single AI answer and others vanish

the gap between brands that read documentation and brands that hire consultants to guess is going to define the next decade of search

the docs are free

the consultants are not

your call

kept.text:
As a brand strategist, my process is the first thing a client experiences. So it has to be right. 

It starts with a discovery call where we talk and see if our values match.. 

From there, I send an onboarding form... 

I take time to understand your brand, your goals, and the difference between what you think you need and what actually moves the needle.

After that i send my invoice and payment is handled seamlessly so we can move without friction.

 Then the work begins.  Research, positioning, strategy, and building clarity around your brand direction. 

I also make sure everything that goes out stays consistent, intentional, and on-brand across the board.

And if one day we have to part ways, we close out with an off-boarding form,  just to understand your experience and keep improving how I work with clients.

A simple process with clear communication and no chaos🙃
